message:
bench(stdio): replace placeholder with real printf + buffered I/O benchmarks

The `stdio_bench.rs` Criterion harness was a single-line placeholder
that benched `1 + 1` so CI had something to compile. Replace it with
three benchmark groups that exercise the actual hot paths exposed by
`frankenlibc_core::stdio`:

- `stdio_printf_parse`: feeds the recognized printf-format inputs
  through `parse_format_string` for three representative shapes —
  a pure literal ("`frankenlibc stdio hot path`"), a mixed
  flag/width/precision conversion line typical of veneer log
  messages ("`fd=%d path=%-24s errno=%#x\n`"), and a positional-arg
  case with `%n$` selectors ("`%2$*1$d:%3$.8s:%4$p`"). Wires
  `Throughput::Bytes(fmt.len())` so Criterion reports MB/s for the
  parser scan rate.
- `stdio_printf_render`: builds two stable `FormatSpec` values
  outside the timing loop — a sign-forcing zero-padded `Ll` signed
  decimal and a left-justified bounded `%s` — and times the
  underlying `format_signed` and `format_str` writers into a
  pre-sized `Vec<u8>`. `Throughput::Elements(1)` makes the unit
  "renders per second", which is the natural metric for these
  one-shot writers.
- New `bench_buffered_stdio` group (added in the same `criterion_group!`
  macro): drives `StdioStream` through `BufMode::Line` and
  `BufMode::Block` configurations using the local
  `write_only_flags()` helper so the benchmark exercises the
  real buffer-flush boundaries in the way the libc-veneer
  callers do.

The new helper `write_only_flags()` returns a canonical
`OpenFlags { writable: true, truncate: true, create: true, .. }`
so each buffer benchmark opens a fresh sink without re-typing the
struct literal. All inputs are wrapped in `black_box` and the
output `Vec`s are captured by `black_box` to defeat dead-code
elimination at `--release`.

The old `bench_placeholder` is retired; `criterion_group!` now
lists `bench_printf_parse, bench_printf_render, bench_buffered_stdio`
so `cargo bench -p frankenlibc-bench --bench stdio_bench` produces
the new families. Existing baselines under `target/criterion/`
covering the placeholder name will no-op cleanly because the new
group names do not collide with the old `stdio_placeholder` ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
